description_long: This is a stylized, dark gray tombstone with a slightly irregular, weathered shape and a broad rectangular base. The front face features a prominent engraving of a skull, adding a somber and slightly ominous character to the monument. The surface is marked with visible cracks and chisel marks, suggesting age and exposure to the elements. The back of the tombstone is plain, lacking any inscriptions or decorative elements. The sides are narrow and show the thickness of the stone, with additional surface wear and texture. The overall appearance is that of a traditional grave marker, likely made from stone, and designed to be a durable memorial in a graveyard setting.
description: A weathered, dark gray tombstone with a skull engraving on the front and a broad rectangular base, featuring visible cracks and chisel marks.
description_view_0: Front view showing the skull engraving and most of the surface cracks and weathering.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the thickness of the tombstone and the irregular, chiseled edges.
description_view_2: Rear view revealing a plain, unadorned surface with some cracks and wear.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, similar to view 1, highlighting the stone's thickness and texture.
